2605.10202.title: Task-Aware Calibration: Provably Optimal Decoding in LLMs
2605.10202.paper_summary: LLM decoding often relies on the model's predictive distribution to generate an output. Consequently, misalignment with respect to the true generating distribution leads to suboptimal decisions in practice. While a natural solution is to calibrate the model's output distribution, for LLMs, this is ill-posed at the combinatorially vast level of free-form language. We address this by building on the insight that in many tasks, these free-form outputs can be interpreted in a semantically meaningful latent structure, for example, discrete class labels, integers, or sets. We introduce task calibration as a paradigm to calibrate the model's predictive distribution in the task-induced latent space. We apply a decision-theoretic result to show that Minimum Bayes Risk (MBR) decoding on the task-calibrated latent distribution is the optimal decoding strategy on latent model beliefs. Empirically, it consistently improves generation quality across different tasks and baselines. We also introduce Task Calibration Error (TCE), an application-aware calibration metric that quantifies the excess loss due to miscalibration. Our work demonstrates that task calibration enables more reliable model decisions across various tasks and applications.
2605.10202.paper_summary_zh: LLM 解碼通常依賴於模型的預測分佈來生成輸出。<lb/>因此，與真實生成分佈的不一致會導致實際中的次優決策。<lb/>雖然一個自然的解決方案是對模型的輸出分佈進行校準，但對於 LLM 而言，這在自由形式語言的組合性龐大層面上是個不適定問題。<lb/>我們通過基於這樣的見解來解決這個問題：在許多任務中，這些自由形式的輸出可以在語義上被解釋為有意義的潛在結構，例如離散類別標籤、整數或集合。<lb/>我們引入任務校準作為一種範式，以在任務誘導的潛在空間中校準模型的預測分佈。<lb/>我們應用一個決策理論結果來顯示，在任務校準的潛在分佈上進行最小貝葉斯風險 (MBR) 解碼是潛在模型信念上的最佳解碼策略。<lb/>在實證上，它在不同任務和基準中始終提高生成質量。<lb/>我們還引入了任務校準誤差 (TCE)，這是一種應用感知的校準指標，用於量化由於校準不當而產生的額外損失。<lb/>我們的工作表明，任務校準使模型在各種任務和應用中能夠做出更可靠的決策。
2605.10202.author: Tim Tomov et.al.
2605.10202.authors: Tim Tomov, Dominik Fuchsgruber, Rajeev Verma, Stephan Günnemann
